questions.text: What is the core theme of the opening verses of Surah Al-Ankabut?
questions.answer: The inevitability of being tested; people will not be left to say 'We believe' without being tried.

questions.text: What is the command regarding parents if they try to force a child into Shirk?
questions.answer: The child should not obey them in that, but should still treat them with goodness in other matters.

questions.text: What will the disbelievers say to the believers regarding their sins (Verse 12)?
questions.answer: They say 'Follow our way, and we will carry your sins,' but they cannot carry any of their sins.

questions.text: How many years did Nuh (AS) remain among his people calling them to Allah?
questions.answer: Nine hundred and fifty years.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) argument against the idols in this Surah?
questions.answer: He told his people they only worship idols and create a falsehood, but these idols possess no provision for them.

questions.text: Who was the only one who believed in Ibrahim from his immediate surroundings?
questions.answer: Lut (AS).

questions.text: What did Ibrahim (AS) say about the idols and the Day of Resurrection (Verse 25)?
questions.answer: He said their mutual love for idols was only for this world, but on the Day of Judgment they will deny and curse each other.

questions.text: Why were the angels sent to the people of Lut?
questions.answer: To destroy the town because its people were wrongdoers and committed immorality (homosexuality) that no one had done before.

questions.text: What were the punishments of the people of 'Ad and Thamud?
questions.answer: They were destroyed despite being people of perception, because they rejected the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'Parable of the Spider' mentioned in Verse 41?
questions.answer: Those who take protectors other than Allah are like the spider that takes a house; indeed, the flimsiest of houses is the spider's house.

questions.text: What is the primary spiritual benefit of Prayer (Salat) mentioned in Verse 45?
questions.answer: It prohibits immorality (Fahsha) and wrongdoing (Munkar).

questions.text: How should believers argue with the People of the Book?
questions.answer: In the best way possible, except with those who do wrong among them.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the life of this world in Verse 64?
questions.answer: As nothing but amusement and play, while the home of the Hereafter is the true life (Al-Hayawan).

questions.text: What is the reaction of the polytheists when they are in a ship in a storm?
questions.answer: They call upon Allah with sincere devotion, but as soon as He brings them safely to land, they associate partners with Him again.

questions.text: What is the promise to those who strive (Jihad) in the way of Allah?
questions.answer: Allah will surely guide them to His paths (Verse 69).

questions.text: Why was the Prophet (SAW) described as not having read any book or written with his right hand before revelation?
questions.answer: So that the followers of falsehood would have no basis for doubt regarding the divine origin of the Quran.

questions.text: Who is the most unjust person according to Verse 68?
questions.answer: The one who invents a lie about Allah or denies the truth when it has come to him.

questions.text: What happened to Korah (Qarun), Pharaoh, and Haman in this Surah?
questions.answer: Musa came to them with clear signs, but they were arrogant on earth; each was seized for his sin.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the 'testing' of the previous nations?
questions.answer: Allah tested those before them so He may make known those who are truthful and those who are liars.

questions.text: What is the 'greatest' thing according to Verse 45?
questions.answer: The remembrance of Allah (Dhikirullah).
